title: Ranking and recommending scientific papers on a complete citation graph: PageRank without the uniformity artifact, and paper-trained transformer embeddings that beat TF-IDF on held-out citation prediction
description: We rebuild an academic paper ranking-and-recommendation system on a complete OpenAlex citation graph (subfield 3106, Nuclear & High Energy Physics, 2015-2024: 199,400 works, 1,942,373 in-corpus citation edges) and add the quantitative evaluation its predecessor (a UMass MA544 course project) lacked. On the complete graph the same power-method PageRank (damping 0.85) becomes heavy-tailed (Gini 0.53, cv 4.19, top-1 work 967x the uniform mass), eliminating the uniformity artifact the predecessor reported. We add SPECTER (paper-trained transformer) sentence embeddings, run on a local AMD GPU via ROCm, and a held-out citation-prediction evaluation (mask 30% of each query's in-corpus references; Recall@k / MAP / MRR with bootstrap 95% CIs). On 2,359 queries SPECTER attains the best MAP (0.063 [0.059, 0.067]) of all five methods, beating the TF-IDF baseline by +15.4% relative MAP (paired-bootstrap p = 0.0005). Limitations (single subfield, in-corpus closed-world candidate pool, GPU-bounded neural sample) are stated plainly. MIT (code) / CC-BY-4.0 (data, via OpenAlex CC0). A DOI will be minted on the next research-atlas release.
